scope.name: ReviewerRegistry
scope.purpose: Define the canonical reviewer registry model that governs role-based approval authority for APPROVAL_LINEAGE_VALID determination. Role membership is explicit — no implicit hierarchy, no inherited authority unless explicitly declared.

reviewer_registry_model.description: Each reviewer entry binds a reviewer identity to an explicit set of roles. No role inheritance or implicit hierarchy is permitted.

reviewer_entry_shape.roles: array of strings — explicit role assignments only

role_resolution.rule: required_role must appear in reviewer.roles. No hierarchy expansion. No inheritance.
role_resolution.inherited_authority_exception: Authority may only be inherited when an explicit declaration is present in the registry entry.

mutation_class_requirements.description: Each mutation class declares the exact roles whose approval satisfies the APPROVAL_LINEAGE requirement for that class. Satisfying the count of approvals from lower roles does not substitute for a required role.
mutation_class_requirements.approval_count_not_role_authority: A mutation class requiring SECURITY_OFFICER approval is not satisfied by any number of MAINTAINER approvals unless SECURITY_OFFICER is explicitly present in the reviewer's role list.

boundary_clarifications.approval_count_is_not_role_authority: Approval count does not substitute for role authority.
boundary_clarifications.security_officer_not_implied_by_maintain: SECURITY_OFFICER ≠ MAINTAINER unless both roles are explicitly assigned.
boundary_clarifications.no_merge_permission_implied: This registry does not create merge permission, authority, execution claims, or deployment eligibility.

deterministic_artifact.rule: Identical reviewer registry + mutation class + approval inputs must produce identical APPROVAL_LINEAGE_VALID or APPROVAL_LINEAGE_INVALID outputs.
